title: validate_dcf.py is duplicated across three plugin skill directories with no shared source
body: The exact same DCFModelValidator implementation appears at plugins/agent-plugins/model-builder/skills/dcf-model/scripts/validate_dcf.py, plugins/agent-plugins/pitch-agent/skills/dcf-model/scripts/validate_dcf.py, and plugins/vertical-plugins/financial-analysis/skills/dcf-model/scripts/validate_dcf.py. A fix to check_dcf_logic in one copy (e.g. handling negative terminal growth rates) has to be manually applied three times or it silently drifts.

title: normalize_number in extract_numbers.py mishandles ambiguous 'M' unit inside a word like 'Margin'
body: detect_category() and normalize_number() in skills/ib-check-deck/scripts/extract_numbers.py both scan the surrounding context string with substring checks like 'm' in unit.lower(); since the multipliers dict includes short keys such as 'M' and 'K', a context snippet mentioning 'Margin' or 'Key metric' can cause the wrong multiplier to be picked for a nearby bare number, inflating extracted values by 1e6 or 1e3 incorrectly.

title: check.py's ensure_hooks_installed() silently overwrites a developer's custom core.hooksPath
body: scripts/check.py runs ensure_hooks_installed() unconditionally on import, which force-sets `git config core.hooksPath` to '.githooks' if it differs from that value. Anyone who has their own hooksPath configured (e.g. for a shared monorepo tooling setup) gets it silently clobbered just by running the plugin manifest linter.

title: DCFModelValidator raises unhelpful traceback when workbook has protected/password-locked sheets
body: In validate_dcf.py, __init__ calls openpyxl.load_workbook(excel_path, data_only=False) and again with data_only=True, but if a sheet is password-protected openpyxl can raise deep inside zipfile/lxml parsing rather than a clean error from DCFModelValidator itself, making it hard for a Claude skill invoking this script to explain the failure to the user.

title: sync-agent-skills.py needs docs on how vertical-plugins and agent-plugins skills get kept in sync
body: Given that dcf-model and ib-check-deck skills are duplicated between plugins/agent-plugins/* and plugins/vertical-plugins/financial-analysis/*, I'd expect scripts/sync-agent-skills.py to be the tool that keeps them consistent, but there's no README/comment explaining whether it copies vertical-plugins into agent-plugins, the reverse, or something else.
